evidence_quotes.quote: Too often videogame publishers sell games on Steam which either are no longer playable due to technical issues they refuse to fix or they simply arbitrarily decide to make the game unplayable after a period of time.
the game Lemnis Gate which was taken down by the developers when they decided to end support for it without giving any means for those who bought the product to play it anymore

paragraphs.text: While I applaud this Act and the spirit behind it I feel it fails to cover a key aspect on digital goods and services, namely videogames. Too often videogame publishers sell games on Steam which either are no longer playable due to technical issues they refuse to fix or they simply arbitrarily decide to make the game unplayable after a period of time. The best example for this is the game Lemnis Gate which was taken down by the developers when they decided to end support for it without giving any means for those who bought the product to play it anymore even though the game was still being sold 1 week before being taken down forever. Videogames are one of the biggest digital goods markets in the EU, netting 84,4 billion euros in 2024 alone. As such I propose that this Act also include some form of stipulation requiring developers or publishers to leave the game in a playable state, for the platform it was released on, when they decide to end support for it.
